title: Deep audit: 3 real bugs fixed (MC annualization, rolling MDD off-by-one, copula dead code)

bugs_found: monte_carlo.py:575 — annual_return_pct uses hardcoded sim_cfg['forecast_years']=5 instead of actual days/252 when forecast_days_override is provided. For a 1-year override, annual return was reported as (1+total)^(1/5)-1 = 1.24% instead of 6.37%.

drawdown_analyzer.py:266 — Rolling max drawdown loop starts at range(window, len(prices)) which produces slice prices[1:window+1], skipping index 0. The first window never includes the first data point, missing drawdowns that start at position 0.

copula_tail.py:262-267 — Dead code in _fit_student_t uses stats.gamma((nu+2)/2).args[0] which creates a gamma DISTRIBUTION object and reads back its shape parameter (not the gamma function). Would produce completely wrong log-likelihood if gammaln import failed.
observation.gap_identified: Math correctness in core simulation and analytics modules — wrong annualization and off-by-one errors silently produce incorrect numbers that pass existing tests.

bugs_fixed: monte_carlo.py: Replaced sim_cfg['forecast_years'] with actual_years = days / trading_days_per_year for annualization. Added max(0.1) guard for sub-year simulations.
drawdown_analyzer.py: Changed rolling MDD loop from range(window, ...) to range(window-1, ...) with index prices.index[window-1:]. First window now correctly covers prices[0:window].

copula_tail.py: Removed 6 lines of dead code (stats.gamma misuse + manual log-likelihood). Moved gammaln import and copula log-likelihood computation to be the primary code path instead of a try/except fallback.

limitations: Audited 8 service files out of 55 — deeper coverage would find more bugs
The MC annualization bug only manifests when forecast_days_override is used (router computes its own annualization correctly)
The drawdown off-by-one only affects the first window — later windows are correct
assessment.self_critique: Should have audited more aggressively wide — checking 10+ files rather than deep-reading 8. The copula dead code fix is low-impact since gammaln never fails in practice. The MC bug is the highest-value find.

next_steps: Audit signal_engine.py get_stock_signal for similar weight/component mismatches
Audit crash_model.py for calibration edge cases (degenerate Platt scaling)
Audit portfolio_engine.py for portfolio construction edge cases
Add integration test for run_monte_carlo with various forecast_days_override values
Audit sector_rotation.py for sector name mismatches between yfinance and config
